steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best uses the notes to explain how the Jules Falk Stradivarius got its name. The answer must connect note 3 (many violins are named for a previous owner) to note 4 (the Jules Falk Stradivarius is named for Jules Falk) to actually explain the naming mechanism.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Jules Falk was an American violinist.': Identifies Jules Falk but gives no information about naming conventions — it doesn't explain how the violin got its name. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Of the 1,000 or so violins Antonio Stradivari made, only about 5000 exist today.': Misrepresents the notes (the notes say ~500 survive, not 5,000) and says nothing about naming. Factually wrong and irrelevant. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Designed by Antonio Stradivari. Stradivarius violins like the Jules Falk are renowned for their quality.': Focuses on the maker's reputation, not the naming reason. Also uses a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — 'The Jules Falk Stradivarius is named after its former owner, Jules Falk.': Applies the naming pattern from note 3 directly to the specific violin from note 4, fully explaining the naming mechanism. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
